title: cordyline excelsa
intro: Cordyline excelsa, also known as the cabbage tree or toromiro, is a species of flowering plant in the asparagus family native to New Zealand. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional Maori medicine, with its leaves, roots, and seeds exhibiting medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The leaves of the Cordyline excelsa plant are rich in antioxidants and have been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, offering health benefits like reducing inflammation and improving immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
